Notes: Small routine scheduled sale at $295.06/share. JPM was near all-time highs. No material event context.

Sold $38.5M in NVDA stock over two days (Mar. 19-20, 2026) as NVIDIA faced new U.S. export restrictions on AI chips to China (announced Mar. 2026) and amid antitrust scrutiny over its dominant position in AI chips. Stevens retains 7.5M+ shares. Large block sale by director during regulatory headwind period.
Small routine sale at $155.50/share. Talley has been making small periodic sales throughout 2025-2026. ExxonMobil was trading near 52-week highs. No unusual circumstances.

Award grant at $0 (RSU grant). No open-market sale or purchase. Bourla's 2026 grant as part of regular executive compensation program. Pfizer continued to trade near multi-year lows amid post-COVID revenue decline.

Note: Duplicate entry corrected — this reflects CVX's Pate transaction above. Wirth's Nov. 2024 exercise of 164,600 shares ($103.71 basis) was a compensation plan transaction, not an open-market sale. Retained all shares after exercise.

Routine scheduled sale at $397/share following option exercise. Taneja regularly sells Tesla shares under 10b5-1 plan after exercising options. Tesla stock had been under pressure from declining deliveries and political controversies surrounding Elon Musk's role in DOGE.

Sold $9M in CVX shares (via exercise + same-day sale) on Mar. 6 as oil price volatility spiked and Chevron was facing litigation over its Hess acquisition. Pate has rarely made large option-exercise sales. Large block sold at $192/share near recent highs while broader energy sector faced pressure from potential demand destruction.

Routine sale at $57.80/share following RSU vest. Moderna stock had been in sustained decline from its 2021 peak of ~$497. Sale was part of standard compensation plan. Klinger retained 65,782 shares after sale.

Tax-withholding RSU vest transaction (F-INKIND) at $83.93/share. Wells Fargo stock near 5-year high as Fed finally lifted major consent order constraints in 2024. Van Beurden retained 259,268 shares after this vest-related transaction.

Nominal RSU vest withholding transaction at $26.93/share. Hoge retained 1,452,119 shares. Moderna was near 5-year lows in 2025-2026 as COVID vaccine revenue normalized and pipeline programs faced delays. Routine compensation plan transaction.

Open-market purchase at $224.20/share — significant director buy after Boeing stock fell ~50% from 2023 highs. Buckley (Vanguard CEO) purchased while Boeing was dealing with manufacturing quality issues, strikes, and the Alaska Airlines door plug blowout aftermath. Insider purchase signals board confidence.

Sold at $745/share — NOC near all-time high. This follows a pattern of multiple NOC executives selling at elevated prices ahead of potential defense budget cuts under the Trump/Musk DOGE initiative in 2026. NOC stock fell substantially in early 2026 as defense spending cuts were debated.
Sold $1.26M in NOC shares at $720/share as Northrop stock recovered following large B-21 bomber contract milestones. Routine executive compensation plan sale. Roeder retained 2,353 shares after sale.

Tax-withholding RSU vest sale at $232.26/share. F-INKIND (withheld for taxes) transaction — not a discretionary sale. Boeing was recovering from 2024 manufacturing crisis and machinists' strike. Shockey retained 23,293 shares.

Scheduled sale following vesting of restricted stock units. Jassy exercised 24,680 restricted shares Feb. 21 and sold a portion two days later at $204.25. Routine scheduled transaction under 10b5-1 plan.

Sold $2.58M in RTX shares at $202.83. RTX stock had reached all-time highs on strong defense spending outlook and Pratt & Whitney engine business recovery. Sale appears to be routine executive compensation plan exercise and sale.

Sold $141K at $727.62/share. Combined with her March sale, Simpson divested significant holdings as defense stocks traded near historic highs just before DOGE-related defense spending cut announcements began to weigh on sector.
Sold $2.85M across three transactions at $950-953/share. GS was near all-time highs after a strong 2025 for investment banking. Rogers retained 49,166 shares. Routine executive compensation sale.

DOJ/SEC charged six people in Dec. 2025 with a $41M insider trading and stock manipulation scheme involving Olema Oncology and Opiant Pharmaceuticals. A former Citigroup investment banker leaked at least 9 nonpublic M&A deals to accomplices who traded ahead of announcements. Also fabricated clinical data to inflate stock prices before selling. 51 charges across 6 defendants.

Open-market purchase at $206.15/share — a bullish insider buy signal. JNJ stock was near multi-year lows amid talc litigation concerns. Director purchases on the open market are generally viewed as positive sentiment indicators.

Sold 7,792 shares at $490-492/share, exhausting his entire position (0.52 shares remaining). Scheduled option exercise and sale following vesting. LMT was near 52-week lows as defense budget debates continued. St. John departed LMT as COO in late 2025.
Nominal sale of 30 shares at $252.16 alongside a small award grant. Routine transaction by independent board chair. No significance beyond routine portfolio management.

Scheduled divestiture under 10b5-1 plan. Huang regularly sells small portions of his enormous NVDA stake (~72M shares). No unusual timing relative to company events. NVDA stock had more than doubled from 2024 lows.

Sold entire position (25,968 shares) at $159.79 — zero shares remaining after sale. Large concentrated single-day sale of all held shares just after RTX disclosed Pratt & Whitney GTF engine inspection program would affect airlines with significant financial liability (est. $3B+). Eddy liquidated fully before market absorbed full P&W impact.

Sold $2M via scheduled option exercise and sale at $90-94/share. RTX's Collins Aerospace unit had been recovering from pandemic-era defense and commercial aerospace disruptions. Brunk retained 24,095 shares after the sale.

Sold $1M via option exercise at $312.93/share. Routine compensation plan sale. GD stock was benefiting from continued DoD budgets and Gulfstream business jet demand. De Leon has served on GD board since 2011.

Sold 3,220 shares at $312.93 following option exercise. GD stock was near multi-year highs on strong defense spending outlook and Gulfstream jet sales. De Leon is a former Deputy Secretary of Defense with longstanding defense industry connections.

Bezos continues to regularly sell Amazon shares under scheduled plans. Sold $50M+ at $223/share. He has sold billions in Amazon stock since stepping down as CEO in 2021, primarily to fund Blue Origin space venture. Retains ~905M+ shares (largest single shareholder).

Sold at $176.27/share in March 2026 (Shah serves on Nvidia board since ~2023). Shah joined Nvidia's board while serving as a senior executive at Eli Lilly. She brings pharma/AI intersection expertise. Her board position gives her advance access to Nvidia's roadmap information. Sale during period of AI chip export restriction uncertainty.
Sold 2,000 shares at $245/share. LHX has been engaged in restructuring after its 2019 merger with L3 Technologies. Zoiss retained 25,325 shares. Routine executive sale under compensation plan.
Scheduled sale at $262.53/share. JPM CRO sale during period when JPM was trading near all-time highs. Part of systematic compensation plan. Retained 234,992 shares.
Sold $3.29M at $263.08/share. Routine executive compensation plan sale. JPM was near all-time highs following strong investment banking and trading revenue. Retained 151,126 shares.

Sold 150,000 shares at $66.67 as CVS stock was in multi-year decline amid structural headwinds in retail pharmacy and insurance divisions. Robbins retained 8.2M+ shares. Large block sale but consistent with his pattern of periodically reducing positions in his board companies.
Sold $10M in JPM shares at $250.77 following RSU vest. Barnum retained 18,017 shares after this planned divestiture. Routine post-vest sale. JPMorgan stock had risen 20%+ in trailing 12 months.

Nominal sale but part of a pattern — Welsh (retired 4-star general and former Air Force Chief of Staff) sold NOC shares systematically while on the board. His position on the board gives him access to sensitive defense contract status information, raising ongoing questions about the revolving door between military leadership and defense contractor boards.
Gift/transfer transaction at $0 — Kubasik donated LHX shares to charitable organization. Not a market sale. Routine philanthropic divestiture. He retained 47,830 shares.

Award grant (A-AWARD) at $25.75/share basis — RSU compensation award, not an open-market purchase. Pfizer was near 10-year lows in early 2025, struggling with post-COVID revenue normalization. Grant was part of retention package for senior leadership.
Routine scheduled sale under 10b5-1 plan. Zuckerberg regularly sells Meta shares to fund philanthropic work through the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative. No unusual timing relative to corporate events.

End-of-year scheduled sale under 10b5-1 plan at ~$606/share. Meta stock had risen 66%+ in 2024 following strong ad revenue recovery. Part of ongoing systematic divestiture to fund the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative.

Gift/transfer transaction at $0 (charitable donation) — Nadella donated MSFT shares to charitable organizations. Not a market sale; reflects typical philanthropic activity by major tech executives. Nadella retained ~859,608 shares.

Scheduled sale at $423.66/share following vesting of compensation shares. Microsoft was near all-time highs driven by Azure AI growth. Retained 103,366 shares after sale. No unusual timing relative to material events.
Sold $909,610 in Citi shares at $69.97. Citi was in the midst of a major restructuring under CEO Jane Fraser. Henry retained 3,022 shares. No unusual timing relative to Citi corporate events.

NVDA board members collectively sold $75M+ in NVDA shares in Q4 2024 as export restrictions on H100/H200 chips to China expanded and new Blackwell GPU demand was generating enormous anticipation. Director Mark Stevens alone sold $38.5M in March 2026 during a period of active regulatory headwinds.

Multiple DJT insiders sought to sell or distribute millions of shares through 2024 as the stock was highly volatile (traded from $10 to $79 and back). Trump's lockup expiration created enormous potential for insider selling. Trump Media filed SEC disclosures for planned sales; stock remains predominantly supported by political sentiment rather than business fundamentals.

Sold over $213M in Amazon shares in a single day at ~$200/share. Part of scheduled plan. Post-2021 CEO transition, Bezos has been systematically reducing his Amazon stake while his net worth rises due to stock appreciation. Retained 926M+ shares.

Scheduled director sale at $199.85/share. Rubinstein (former Palm CEO and Apple hardware SVP) has been an Amazon director since 2010. Routine annual divestiture of vested director compensation. Amazon was near all-time highs on AWS cloud growth and advertising revenue.

Large scheduled option exercise and sale at $303.26 following RSU vest. Gallopoulos is GD's top lawyer and retained 106,952 shares after the sale. GD was near 52-week highs on strong defense and Gulfstream aviation orders.
Gift/G-GIFT transaction at $0 — De Leon donated 248 GD shares to charitable organization. Not a market sale. Routine philanthropic activity.

Sold 4,000 shares at $250/share following option exercise. Routine scheduled sale. Tesla was recovering from 2024 demand concerns but had not yet announced major new models. Taneja retained 105,032 shares.

Sold $84.9M in shares Oct. 17, one week after the company reportedly received notice of a DOJ antitrust investigation. Stock dropped 5.2% over the two days after the investigation became public (Feb. 27-28, 2025). Bloomberg reported no evidence transactions were made under scheduled 10b5-1 plans. Sen. Elizabeth Warren called for SEC investigation.

Sold $1.09M on Oct. 16, the week after UnitedHealth reportedly received DOJ antitrust investigation notice. Shares fell after investigation became public. Bloomberg reported this was her first sale in years, and was not part of a disclosed 10b5-1 plan. Sen. Warren flagged trading as suspicious.
Sold $3.82M in WFC shares at $63.70. Wells Fargo was recovering from years of regulatory consent orders. Williams sold prior to WFC's strong Q4 earnings. Retained 163,609 shares.

Sold $450,000 days after UNH reportedly received DOJ antitrust probe notice. Bloomberg reported no indication of a 10b5-1 scheduled plan. Part of the $101.5M in combined executive sales by four UNH insiders before the probe became public knowledge.
Sold $2.17M in WFC shares at $61.99. Van Beurden retained 126,687 shares. Routine scheduled sale. WFC was trading near its highest level since 2019 consent order was partially lifted.

Sold 155,000 shares at $132/share, followed by 125,000 more shares Oct. 3 at $122/share — during a period when NVIDIA faced initial U.S. chip export restrictions to China (Oct. 2023 and 2024 expansions). Total 280,000 shares sold within two weeks, $35.8M. Timing correlated with escalating regulatory pressure on AI chip exports.

Sold $3.76M in Google stock on Oct. 2, 2024 — while the DOJ antitrust monopoly trial was ongoing (ruling came Aug. 2024, finding Google an illegal monopoly). Though under a 10b5-1 plan, the timing during active antitrust proceedings and DOJ remedies discussions drew media attention. Stock was near all-time highs.

Annual scheduled sale under 10b5-1 plan at $226.57/share. Cook's sale was consistent with his pattern of annual late-Q3/early-Q4 divestiture. Apple had just released iPhone 16 lineup. No unusual timing.

Scheduled annual sale at $226-227/share. Part of O'Brien's systematic annual divestiture plan. Apple's retail strategy and workforce management functions are her domain. No unusual timing or corporate events at time of sale.

SEC announced in FY2024 multiple insider trading cases resulting from its AI-enhanced market surveillance identifying unusual trading patterns. SEC's Division of Enforcement specifically highlighted cases involving clinical trial MNPI, M&A deal leakage, and trading around earnings disclosures. SEC recorded $8.2B in total remedies in FY2024, its highest in history.

Sold $5.6M on same day a ransomware attack struck Change Healthcare, wiping out $46B+ in market cap in ensuing weeks. Professor Richard Painter (UMN Law) said publicly: 'He sold the shares on the same day he learned of the ransomware attack.' Stock fell from $521.97 to $439.20 by Apr. 12, 2024.

Sold $4.4M on the same day (Feb. 21, 2024) that a ransomware attack struck UnitedHealth's Change Healthcare subsidiary. Executives claimed these were tax-withholding transactions tied to RSU vesting, not discretionary sales — but experts noted they chose to execute on one of the worst possible days from a fiduciary standpoint.

Sold $4.4M on the same day UnitedHealth's Change Healthcare was hit with a ransomware attack. Combined with CEO Witty, CFO Rex, and other C-suite executives selling the same day, total sales were $17.7M. McMahon subsequently left the company.

CLO sold $750K on same day as ransomware attack on Change Healthcare. Bondy's signature would typically be required for compliance on executive stock sales — meaning the CLO approved his own sale on the day of a material adverse event. Adds to scrutiny of UNH trading that day.

Thompson sold $1.5M on Feb. 21, 2024 — the day of the Change Healthcare ransomware attack. He also sold $15.1M on Feb. 16, 2024 — 11 days before the DOJ antitrust investigation became public. Thompson was later assassinated in New York City on Dec. 4, 2024. Both sales drew congressional and SEC scrutiny.

Sold $15.1M on Feb. 16 — 11 days before the DOJ antitrust investigation was publicly reported. Also sold $5.6M on Feb. 21, 2024 — the same day UnitedHealth's Change Healthcare subsidiary was hit by a ransomware attack. Thompson was later assassinated in December 2024. Sales preceded major stock declines.

Calhoun announced he was selling shares worth ~$12M under a 10b5-1 plan as Boeing faced mounting crisis. On Jan. 5, an Alaska Airlines 737 MAX 9 door plug blew out mid-flight. The company was concurrently under criminal investigation and DOJ monitoring. Calhoun's sale was pre-planned but the timing amid the escalating safety crisis drew intense media scrutiny.

Paul Pelosi (husband of then-Speaker Nancy Pelosi) sold 30,000 Google shares in three transactions Dec. 20-28, 2022 — one month before the DOJ and eight states filed antitrust lawsuit against Google on Jan. 24, 2023. No explanation given for the timing. Combined trades totaled $1.5-3M. Fox Business first reported the suspicious timing.

SEC charged Wang with insider trading after he allegedly purchased C4 shares while performing biostatistical consulting work and gaining advance knowledge of positive clinical trial results for C4's flagship cancer drug. Bought shares Nov.-Dec. 2023, before company announced positive results Dec. 12. Made $489,739 in profits. Criminal charges filed in parallel by DOJ (Jan. 2026).

First tranche of purchases between Nov. 20 and Dec. 12, 2023, while Wang was performing biostatistical analysis on C4's clinical trial data and had advance knowledge of positive results. SEC noted Wang used four separate brokerage accounts to disguise trading pattern. C4 announced positive results Dec. 12; stock surged. Wang charged criminally Jan. 2026.

Winfrey sold her ~1.2M shares ($8M) and resigned from WW's board in February 2024 — just before WW's stock crashed further (~80% drop in 2024) as GLP-1 weight-loss drugs (Ozempic/Wegovy) devastated WW's traditional diet subscription model. Winfrey simultaneously became a prominent spokesperson for GLP-1 drugs. Critics noted she had financial incentives to exit before the GLP-1 disruption fully hit WW.

Huang sold ~$220M in Nvidia shares in 2023 as AI spending boom drove NVDA stock from $150 to $450+. His sales were under 10b5-1 plans but the scale ($220M+) while simultaneously being the face of the AI revolution and making public statements about AI demand drew investor scrutiny about whether timing maximized personal wealth extraction.

Pichai sold Google shares in Jan. 2023 while Google was facing growing DOJ antitrust scrutiny. The DOJ antitrust search monopoly case was formally ruled on in August 2024 (Google found to be an illegal monopoly). Paul Pelosi had sold $1.5-3M in Google shares weeks before the Jan. 24, 2023 DOJ lawsuit was filed.

SEC charged Boeing and Muilenburg with making materially misleading statements to investors following 737 MAX crashes. Boeing settled for $200M penalty; Muilenburg settled for $1M. SEC found they made misleading public statements about aircraft safety while internally aware of MCAS deficiencies, violating antifraud provisions. Boeing stock fell 22% during the crisis.

Cohen sold his entire Bed Bath & Beyond position in August 2022 via call options exercise and stock sale — just weeks after publicly filing upbeat 13-D letters that many retail investors interpreted as bullish. Stock had spiked on meme-driven enthusiasm. Cohen's exit triggered a 40% stock collapse in one day. BBBY filed for bankruptcy in 2023. SEC settled with Cohen for $10M over inadequate disclosure of his intent to sell.

First crypto insider trading prosecution. Wahi used his knowledge of upcoming Coinbase listing announcements to buy tokens before they were listed (token prices typically spike on listing). He and accomplices made ~$1.5M. Convicted in 2023; sentenced to 2 years. SEC also filed civil charges, asserting several tokens were securities — a landmark expansion of crypto enforcement.

Musk filed a late Form 13-D disclosure for his Twitter stake acquisition — he was legally required to disclose within 10 days of crossing 5% ownership but waited over 30 days, trading during the delay at lower prices. SEC settled with Musk and Tesla for $40M+ in 2022. Musk also agreed to 20 days then later backed out of purchase agreement, sparking additional litigation.

Sold $450M+ in Meta shares in early 2022 just before Meta's Q4 2021 earnings revealed a dramatic earnings miss, with daily active users declining for the first time and stock plunging 26% in after-hours trading on Feb. 2. Zuckerberg sold ~$4.5B in Meta stock in 2021-22 total. Though under 10b5-1 plan, the size and timing drew congressional scrutiny.

SEC filed multiple insider trading actions in 2022-2024 related to pharmaceutical executives trading on nonpublic clinical trial information. The agency noted particular vigilance around biotech companies where MNPI about trial outcomes gives executives a significant informational advantage over public investors.

Musk sold another $9.6B in Tesla shares in December 2021, bringing total 2021 sales to ~$16B. He was simultaneously negotiating to purchase Twitter, creating potential conflicts of interest regarding Tesla capital usage. SEC probed whether Musk filed required disclosures on time regarding his Twitter stake acquisition (Form 13-D late filing led to $40M+ settlement).

After tweeting a poll asking followers whether he should sell 10% of his Tesla stake, Musk sold $5B+ in TSLA shares in November 2021 near Tesla's all-time high. Later SEC filings revealed Musk had previously set up 10b5-1 plans. However, SEC investigation found he had been planning to sell before the tweet. Used Twitter poll to create a public rationale, but had already begun preparation. Tesla stock subsequently fell 65% from its Nov. 2021 peak.

Amazon, Ford, and Goldman Sachs — Rivian's major pre-IPO backers — sold $1.5B+ in shares via secondary offerings just months after Rivian's November 2021 IPO as production challenges became apparent. Lockup expirations were used to execute massive sales while retail investors still held inflated post-IPO expectations. Rivian stock fell 85% from IPO peak.

Afeyan sold hundreds of millions worth of Moderna stock throughout 2021 as the stock approached $500. Insider sales by Moderna founders and executives during peak COVID-era stock prices totaled over $1B company-wide. Congressional investigations examined whether 10b5-1 plan amendments were improperly timed.

Sold $28M+ near Moderna's peak (~$380-400/share) during period of peak COVID vaccine demand. Multiple 10b5-1 plan amendments preceded sales near price peaks. Moderna was the primary beneficiary of NIH technology and government vaccine contracts. Critics argued the plan amendments effectively enabled 'legal insider trading.'

Emergent BioSolutions executives sold millions in stock in 2020-2021 while the company held a coveted sole-source contract to produce Johnson & Johnson and AstraZeneca COVID vaccines. In April 2021, it was revealed that Emergent's plant contaminated 15M doses of J&J vaccine and all 62M doses were put on hold. Executives had sold $18M+ in the weeks/months before the contamination became public.

Hwang used total return swaps to accumulate $100B+ in highly leveraged positions while concealing his concentration from counterparties. When positions unwound in March 2021, Credit Suisse lost $5.5B, Nomura ~$3B, and others. Hwang was convicted of racketeering and fraud in July 2024. While not traditional Form 4 insider trading, case represents one of the most damaging concealed trading schemes in history.

Cohen's RC Ventures purchased 9.98M GME shares before publicly pressuring GameStop to transform into a tech company, driving retail investor mania. Cohen sold 6.05M shares in August 2022 — after the stock had been pumped by retail frenzy and while his presence was still publicly bullish. Settled SEC charges for $10M in January 2024 for failing to disclose intent to sell when buying.

Former FDA Commissioner Scott Gottlieb joined Pfizer's board in 2019 and began selling shares after the COVID vaccine announcement. The revolving door between FDA and Pfizer's board while the agency was evaluating Pfizer's vaccine EUA raised conflict-of-interest concerns from congressional Democrats and watchdog groups.

Sold 61.8% of directly held shares on same day Pfizer announced its COVID-19 vaccine was >90% effective, sending stock up ~15%. Trading plan (10b5-1) was adopted Aug. 19 — the day after Pfizer issued a press release confirming it was on track to seek regulatory review of the vaccine. Timing of plan adoption drew significant scrutiny from lawmakers and watchdog groups.

One of multiple large Moderna stock sales by Bancel and other executives during the COVID vaccine development period in 2020-2021, when the company's stock surged from ~$20 to nearly $500. NPR investigation found executives amended their 10b5-1 plans multiple times, often just before material announcements, raising legal questions even as company defended the trades.

Between January and August 2020, Bancel sold $38M worth of Moderna stock during a period of highly positive vaccine announcements. NPR found that on several occasions, Bancel and other executives amended their 10b5-1 plans just before stock-moving announcements. Sales raised suspicions of trades made on confidential information about vaccine trial progress.

Purchased 95,000+ shares just before July 28, 2020 announcement of $765M U.S. government loan to Kodak for pharmaceutical manufacturing. Stock surged from $2.62 to $60 in two days. Stiles had access to nonpublic information about the pending government contract. He and cousin Gray Stiles made $1.5M+. Both pleaded guilty to securities fraud in April 2024.

Tipped by cousin Andrew Stiles about nonpublic information regarding Kodak's $765M government loan. Purchased shares day before announcement. Stock went from $2.62 to $60 in two days. Made $700,000+ in illegal profits. Pleaded guilty to securities fraud April 2024.

Kodak's Chairman received stock options and purchased shares in the days before the July 28, 2020 announcement of a $765M government loan. Congressional investigators flagged unusual trading activity by Kodak insiders in the days preceding the public announcement, including option grants and purchases that would be greatly enriched by the loan announcement. SEC investigated.

After Hertz filed for bankruptcy in May 2020, it attempted to issue $500M in new stock despite being in bankruptcy — exploiting the Reddit/retail investor frenzy that had briefly driven up Hertz stock. The SEC blocked the offering. SEC filed charges against Hertz's advisors for attempting to deceive investors about the company's financial state during the bizarre post-bankruptcy stock squeeze.

Yuan sold ~$100M+ in Zoom shares in 2020-2021 as the stock soared from $70 to $559 during COVID-driven video conferencing boom. He sold at peak valuations while the company was simultaneously dealing with 'Zoomboming' security issues and privacy scandal that it initially failed to fully disclose to users. Under 10b5-1 plans but scale of peak-timing sales drew scrutiny.

Position: U.S. Senator / Senate Intelligence Committee Chair
Notes: Burr sold $1.7M in stock on Feb. 13, 2020 after receiving a classified COVID-19 intelligence briefing as Intel Committee chair. He was among the first Americans to know the full scope of the coming pandemic. Sold hotel stocks and other vulnerable holdings. DOJ investigation was dropped, but he was referred to SEC. Burr did not seek re-election.

Four U.S. Senators — Richard Burr (R-NC), Kelly Loeffler (R-GA), David Perdue (R-GA), and James Inhofe (R-OK) — sold millions in stock after receiving a private Senate COVID briefing on Feb. 24, 2020, and before markets crashed. Burr sold $1.7M; Loeffler sold millions. DOJ investigated Burr (dropped); Loeffler and Perdue faced SEC scrutiny. Highlighted need for STOCK Act enforcement.

Loeffler and husband Jeffrey Sprecher (NYSE Chairman) sold $3.1M+ in stock starting Feb. 24, 2020 — same day as classified Senate COVID briefing. Sold stock in companies likely to be hurt by pandemic; bought stock in protective equipment firms. SEC declined to charge but scrutiny contributed to her 2021 Senate runoff loss.

Neumann cashed out $700M+ through share sales and loans against his WeWork stake before the company's failed IPO in September 2019. He sold stock back to WeWork, borrowed against shares, and transferred assets to entities he personally controlled — all while WeWork was burning through investor capital at enormous rates. SEC investigated; SoftBank and investors suffered billions in losses.

Insider: Jenny Qian Zhiya (CEO) & Charles Zhengyao Lu (Chairman)
Notes: Luckin executives (and shareholder SoftBank) sold $400M+ in shares via block trades in January 2020 at inflated prices while concealing massive fabricated revenue. The company had been inflating sales data since 2019. Stock collapsed 80% after internal whistleblower memo went public in April 2020. SEC charged company in 2020, which settled for $180M.

Boeing CFO sold significant stock during period when the company was publicly minimizing the safety implications of the 737 MAX MCAS system — which had already caused one fatal crash (Oct. 2018). The second crash occurred in March 2019. SEC's 2022 action against Boeing found misleading investor disclosures during this period.

SEC found Boeing and Muilenburg made materially misleading statements following the Oct. 2018 Lion Air crash and before the March 2019 Ethiopian Airlines crash (both 737 MAX). Muilenburg sold millions while publicly insisting the MAX was safe and not disclosing known MCAS documentation gaps. Settled SEC fraud charges for $1M penalty in 2022.

SEC charged Ying with insider trading. Ying exercised all vested Equifax stock options and sold shares before public breach disclosure, avoiding $117,000 in losses. Ying pleaded guilty in 2019, sentenced to 4 months in prison and paid $117,000 in disgorgement plus $55,000 fine.

Sold shares on Aug. 2, four days after breach was detected internally. Equifax Special Committee found he requested preclearance Aug. 1 (after breach was detected), which was granted same day. No 10b5-1 plan listed. Stock later fell ~35% after public disclosure.

Sold shares Aug. 1-2, 2017 — three days after Equifax's cybersecurity team identified suspicious network activity on July 29. The data breach affecting 147 million consumers was not disclosed publicly until Sept. 7. Gamble received legal preclearance from Equifax before selling. The SEC and DOJ investigated all three executives; Jun Ying (CIO-in-waiting) was later convicted.

Sold shares on Aug. 1, just three days after cybersecurity team detected breach. No 10b5-1 plan was in place. Equifax said executives had 'no knowledge' of the breach at time of sale, but the Special Committee report revealed Loughran requested preclearance the day before breach was detected. DOJ launched criminal investigation.

Collins sat on board of Innate Immunotherapeutics. When CEO emailed board about failed MS drug trial, Collins immediately called his son Cameron, who sold nearly all his shares before results went public. Stock dropped 92%. Collins was convicted in 2019 for insider trading and lying to FBI, sentenced to 26 months in prison.

SEC charged Panuwat with insider trading in a 'shadow trading' first: he learned about Pfizer's acquisition of his employer Medivation and instead of trading Medivation stock, he bought call options in Incyte Corp — a peer company likely to rise on the same M&A wave. After Pfizer-Medivation deal announced, Incyte stock surged 8% and Panuwat made $107K. Landmark 'shadow trading' case; jury found him liable 2024.

Three top Seres executives sold a combined $2.5M in shares in the two days before the company announced its microbiome drug had failed a Phase 2 clinical trial, sending stock down ~70%. The sales were made under 10b5-1 plans but STAT News investigation found the timing raised serious questions. Executives avoided ~$2M in paper losses collectively.

Bill Ackman's Pershing Square accumulated a 9.7% stake in Allergan using nonpublic knowledge that Valeant Pharmaceuticals was planning a takeover bid. Ackman made $2.6B profit when the position was eventually sold. SEC investigated the scheme; a court ruled the trading was legal because Pershing Square was Valeant's 'partner' not a traditional corporate insider — created the 'Ackman loophole' in insider trading law.

Martoma obtained nonpublic clinical trial data for an Alzheimer's drug (bapineuzumab) from a neurologist consulting for Elan and Wyeth. SAC sold $960M in Elan and Wyeth positions and shorted the stocks. Drug failed. SAC made $276M in gains/avoided losses. Martoma convicted 2014, sentenced to 9 years. The largest insider trading scheme in U.S. history at the time.

SAC Capital paid $1.8B in criminal and civil penalties — the largest ever in insider trading. The firm systematically cultivated corporate insiders for MNPI across many public companies. Cohen himself was banned from managing outside money for two years. The Martoma case (Elan/Wyeth) was part of this broader SAC insider trading enterprise.

SEC charged Walters with insider trading based on tips from Thomas Davis, a Dean Foods board member. Walters made $17.1M in 2012 trading on nonpublic information that Dean Foods would spin off its WhiteWave unit. Also tipped golfer Phil Mickelson, who made $931K. Walters was convicted in 2017, sentenced to 5 years in prison.

SEC alleged Mickelson purchased Dean Foods stock after receiving a tip from Billy Walters, to whom he owed gambling debt. Made $931K in profits. Was not criminally charged but agreed to pay back $1.03M (including interest) in ill-gotten gains to settle SEC civil action. Walters allegedly urged Mickelson to buy shares before announcement of WhiteWave spin-off.

Rajaratnam built an extensive network of corporate insiders across Goldman Sachs, McKinsey, Intel, IBM, and others to trade on MNPI. Made $63.8M in illegal profits. Convicted in 2011 on 14 counts; sentenced to 11 years in prison (then-longest sentence for insider trading). Case transformed SEC enforcement; $157M in total disgorgement/penalties.

McGuire paid a then-record $468M SEC settlement for options backdating scheme spanning 1994-2005. He backdated stock option grants to coincide with historical low prices, inflating compensation. The scheme involved over a dozen years of deception. Separately paid $30M in forfeitures. One of the largest individual enforcement actions in SEC history.
